title: AI Data Centre Campus
prompt: 500MW AI hyperscale data centre campus in Riyadh, 4 buildings, liquid cooling, 132kV grid substation, concept stage, concern: grid connection lead time, schedule and cost certainty
executive_summary: Digital Infrastructure / Hyperscale Data Centre scenario view: Base. CASEY indicates $8.1B P50 exposure, $6.8B - $11.9B range, 66 months baseline, Medium-High risk and 66% confidence. Confidence is governed by utility energisation, cooling readiness, transformer supply and integrated systems testing. Trade-off: Balanced cost, time and evidence posture.
confidence_explanation: Class 3 Budget Authorization: 10-40% definition maturity drives the starting confidence level.
Schedule Level 4: more detailed schedule logic improves QSRA traceability.
Location/space factor: Saudi Arabia / GCC applies a 1.35 delivery premium.
Benchmark memory matched Digital Infrastructure / Hyperscale Data Centre with similarity score 9.
Cost calibrated by blending CASEY template output with benchmark anchor $3.8B.
Schedule calibrated against benchmark duration 46 months.
Standard complexity profile inferred from project type.

estimate_quality_index.meaning: Confidence index reflects estimate class, schedule level, benchmark similarity, input quality and risk exposure.

procurement_heatmap.reason: Procurement path drives cost and schedule confidence.
Utility readiness controls commissioning and phasing.
Integration maturity is a common late-stage failure mode.
Limited supplier base increases price and schedule volatility.
Transformers, switchgear and grid connection can dominate critical path.

critical_path_narrative: Critical path is likely governed by definition maturity, procurement release and systems commissioning.
Early schedule confidence depends on locking scope, approvals and long-lead package strategy.
For digital infrastructure, grid connection and electrical equipment procurement are likely critical path drivers.
First schedule control focus should be: Requirements definition and scope freeze.
board_briefing: Power availability and systems integration readiness are more likely to govern delivery than shell construction progress.
Base scenario is selected; all cards, narratives, QCRA/QSRA and exports are stamped from this scenario payload.
Base scenario: $8.1B P50, $6.8B - $11.9B range, 66 months baseline and 66% confidence.
Confidence is governed by utility energisation, cooling readiness, transformer supply and integrated systems testing.

uncertainty_narrative.estimate_maturity: Class 3 maturity is suitable for budget authorization, but procurement and design assumptions still need challenge.
uncertainty_narrative.schedule_maturity: Schedule Level 4 gives stronger logic and QSRA traceability.
uncertainty_narrative.interpretation: Live calibration is weighting energisation, cooling readiness and integrated systems testing into the QSRA/QCRA tail.

mission_control_cards.label: Live calibration
mission_control_cards.signal: Current sector conditions are being applied to confidence, contingency and delivery-tail exposure.

mission_control_cards.label: Grid connection congestion
mission_control_cards.signal: Utility interconnection and energisation remain the dominant delivery constraints.

mission_control_cards.label: Transformer / switchgear lead times
mission_control_cards.signal: Long-lead electrical packages shape procurement tail exposure.

mission_control_cards.label: Liquid cooling readiness
mission_control_cards.signal: Cooling readiness and heat-rejection capacity govern commissioning risk.

mission_control_cards.label: IST and phased hall turnover
mission_control_cards.signal: Integrated systems testing and phased data-hall readiness drive confidence.

casey_thinking: CASEY has re-cut the programme as a Base scenario inside the Digital Infrastructure / Hyperscale Data Centre delivery environment. The governing consequence is: confidence is governed by utility energisation, cooling readiness, transformer supply and integrated systems testing. QCRA/QSRA curves, cost basis, risk probabilities and schedule logic are locked to this scenario.
calibration_notes: Benchmark memory matched Digital Infrastructure / Hyperscale Data Centre with similarity score 9.
Cost calibrated by blending CASEY template output with benchmark anchor $3.8B.
Schedule calibrated against benchmark duration 46 months.

risks.title: Grid connection delay
risks.cause: Utility agreement, substation scope or energisation pathway not secured
risks.risk_event: Power connection or energisation milestone slips
risks.impact_description: Critical path delay, temporary power cost, phased opening risk and revenue deferral
risks.description: Power connection or energisation milestone slips
risks.mitigation: Secure grid agreement, temporary power strategy, early equipment orders
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $323M / $970M / $2.1B based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 01.04 Cooling plant.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 25/90/240 days mapped to activity A1400 Planning, regulatory and stakeholder approvals.

risks.title: Market escalation
risks.cause: Supplier market tightness, inflation, FX and long procurement window
risks.risk_event: Rates exceed assumed escalation profile
risks.impact_description: P50 cost becomes understated; commercial approvals or procurement strategy may need reset
risks.description: Rates exceed assumed escalation profile
risks.mitigation: Early procurement, index-linked allowances, market testing, FX strategy
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $323M / $808M / $1.6B based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 02.02 Programme management and procurement.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 0/0/0 days mapped to activity A1700 Long-lead procurement.

risks.title: Design maturity gap
risks.cause: Estimate is based on immature design, incomplete surveys or unresolved interfaces
risks.risk_event: Design basis changes during detailed design
risks.impact_description: Cost growth, quantity movement, rework and schedule resequencing
risks.description: Design basis changes during detailed design
risks.mitigation: Design maturity gates, independent review, assumption register
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $242M / $727M / $1.5B based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 03.01 Risk reserve.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 15/45/120 days mapped to activity A1900 Main construction / fabrication / assembly.

risks.title: Scope growth
risks.cause: Ambiguous requirements, immature scope freeze and late stakeholder changes
risks.risk_event: Approved scope expands after estimate freeze
risks.impact_description: Additional quantities, redesign, rework, procurement churn and board contingency drawdown
risks.description: Approved scope expands after estimate freeze
risks.trigger: Scope change rate exceeds 2% of package value
risks.mitigation: Freeze scope baseline, change board, value gates, client decision log
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $242M / $647M / $1.3B based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 02.01 Commissioning and integrated systems testing.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 15/45/120 days mapped to activity A1600 Detailed design / IFC development.

risks.title: Cooling system capacity
risks.cause: Thermal basis, water availability or cooling vendor performance uncertain
risks.risk_event: Cooling design fails capacity, redundancy or commissioning test
risks.impact_description: Re-design, commissioning delay, lower IT load and resilience concern
risks.description: Cooling design fails capacity, redundancy or commissioning test
risks.mitigation: Thermal modelling, supplier validation, early performance test
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $202M / $647M / $1.4B based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 01.05 Fibre, controls and security.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 10/45/130 days mapped to activity A1500 Procurement strategy and tender packaging.

risks.title: Productivity underperformance
risks.cause: Access constraints, poor sequencing, labour scarcity or learning curve
risks.risk_event: Actual production rates underperform plan
risks.impact_description: Extended duration, increased preliminaries and loss of float
risks.description: Actual production rates underperform plan
risks.trigger: SPI/CPI deteriorates for two reporting periods
risks.mitigation: Package productivity controls, daily planning, earned value cadence
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $242M / $647M / $1.3B based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 03.01 Risk reserve.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 15/50/130 days mapped to activity A2100 Commissioning, operations readiness and closeout.

risks.title: Supply chain delay
risks.cause: Long-lead equipment, constrained suppliers or late procurement release
risks.risk_event: Supplier promise dates move right
risks.impact_description: Delayed installation, critical path movement and acceleration cost
risks.description: Supplier promise dates move right
risks.mitigation: Alternate suppliers, early orders, expediting, framework options
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $162M / $566M / $1.2B based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 03.01 Risk reserve.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 10/40/120 days mapped to activity A2000 Systems integration and testing.

risks.title: Interface misalignment
risks.cause: Multiple contractors, unclear interface ownership or late design coordination
risks.risk_event: Package interfaces do not align during installation/integration
risks.impact_description: Rework, claims, delay and fragmented accountability
risks.description: Package interfaces do not align during installation/integration
risks.mitigation: Interface control documents, weekly interface board, accountable owners
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $162M / $566M / $1.1B based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 03.01 Risk reserve.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 10/35/100 days mapped to activity P2000 Data halls.

risks.title: Commissioning delay
risks.cause: Incomplete readiness, software integration issues or defects during testing
risks.risk_event: Systems fail tests or require repeated commissioning cycles
risks.impact_description: Delayed handover, operational readiness slippage and liquidated damages exposure
risks.description: Systems fail tests or require repeated commissioning cycles
risks.mitigation: Commissioning readiness plan, early test packs, digital systems integration
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $162M / $485M / $970M based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 03.01 Risk reserve.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 10/35/100 days mapped to activity P1000 Grid connection.

risks.title: Permits / approvals delay
risks.cause: Authority process, stakeholder objections or incomplete submissions
risks.risk_event: Consent milestone slips beyond baseline
risks.impact_description: Critical path delay, extended preliminaries and potential redesign conditions
risks.description: Consent milestone slips beyond baseline
risks.mitigation: Authority plan, consent tracker, early submissions, stakeholder map
risks.basis_of_cost_impact: O/M/P cost impact equals $81M / $323M / $647M based on scenario-adjusted exposure to CBS 03.01 Risk reserve.
risks.basis_of_schedule_impact: O/M/P schedule impact equals 20/70/180 days mapped to activity A1800 Site / launch integration readiness.

monte_carlo.curve_interpretation: Reference curve: P50 equals the headline estimate; P80/P90 show normal board-level contingency exposure.
curve_readout: Cost P50 reconciles to $8.1B; P80 is $10.5B; P90 is $11.9B.
Schedule P50 reconciles to 66 months; P80 is 86 months; P90 is 97 months.
Selected scenario curve is locked to the same P50/P80/P90 used by the UI and exports.

benchmarks.why: Varies by power density, grid scope, cooling, land and regional constraints.
Driven by grid connection, long-lead electrical equipment and phased fit-out.
Grid connection is frequently the dominant schedule and risk driver.

scenario_comparison.label: Faster
scenario_comparison.why: Parallel design/procurement, acceleration, premium logistics and higher interface risk.

scenario_comparison.label: Cheaper
scenario_comparison.why: Value engineering, tighter scope and procurement competition, with schedule/quality exposure.

scenario_comparison.label: Lower Risk
scenario_comparison.why: More surveys, assurance, stage gates, contingency and schedule buffers.

scenario_comparison.label: Premium
scenario_comparison.why: Best assurance, independent review, early procurement and high-confidence governance.

scenario_comparison.label: Investor
scenario_comparison.why: Investment-framing case with transparent risk and commercial challenge.

scenario_comparison.label: Survival
scenario_comparison.why: Minimum viable scope, warning-heavy, high residual risk.

launch_demo_script: Open with: 'Give me any project on Earth or in Space.'
Type the project into CASEY and hit Generate Full Intelligence Pack.
Show the executive dashboard: cost, schedule, risk, confidence and top drivers.
Switch to Monte Carlo: show QCRA/QSRA P-curves and tornado drivers linked to activities.
Show scenarios: Faster, Cheaper, Lower Risk and Premium with why each changed.
Open Output Centre: Excel, Risk CSV, XER, cost workbook, risk register, schedule export and full pack are ready.
Close with: 'Traditional advisory takes weeks. CASEY gives the first board-grade view in seconds.'
red_flags: Data centre: grid connection and cooling basis must be validated early.
board_challenge_questions: What evidence supports the P50 cost and P80 confidence?
Which five risks create most contingency?
Which activities drive the QSRA finish-date risk?
What data would increase confidence before approval?
What is the fastest credible lower-risk path?
next_best_actions: Run a focused evidence workshop on the dominant schedule and commissioning constraint.
Validate the top five CBS cost drivers against benchmark or supplier evidence.
Confirm the Level 3/4 schedule logic, handover gates and critical-path assumptions.
Prepare Base / Faster / Cheaper / Lower-Risk decision paper with explicit trade-offs.
Create a confidence-improvement plan targeting design maturity, procurement evidence and operational readiness.

executive_shock_insight: Power availability and systems integration readiness are more likely to govern delivery than shell construction progress.
scenario_contradiction: Acceleration can bring revenue online earlier, but usually buys schedule by paying procurement premiums and accepting commissioning overlap.
sector_confidence_drivers: Scope maturity: campus power and white-space definition
Procurement certainty: transformers, generators and switchgear
Schedule maturity: grid and commissioning logic
Interface exposure: utilities, fibre and commissioning
sector_primary_cost_drivers: Data halls and white space fit-out
why_casey_generated_this: CASEY detected Digital Infrastructure / Hyperscale Data Centre from the project brief and locked the sector ontology before generating outputs.
Sector-native behaviours applied: Transformer lead-time, Grid energisation, Liquid cooling readiness, IST congestion, Commissioning overlap.
Benchmark cohort, causal chain, confidence drivers and risk language were constrained to the selected sector.
The output is designed for early board challenge and scope definition, not certified pricing.

scenario_comparison_vs_base.plain_english: Base versus Base: cost 8.1B to 8.1B, schedule 66 to 66 months, confidence 66% to 66%.

scenario_matrix.label: Base
scenario_matrix.why: Balanced cost, time and evidence posture.; Maintains a credible reference case for board challenge.; Does not buy extra time certainty or capital efficiency.

scenario_matrix.label: Faster
scenario_matrix.why: You bought time by spending money and consuming recovery float.; Earlier revenue / market-entry option and stronger strategic timing.; commissioning validation float, interface stability, procurement optionality and late-stage recovery.

scenario_matrix.label: Cheaper
scenario_matrix.why: You cut capital authorization by moving risk into resilience, redundancy and start-up.; Lower initial approval number and reduced near-term capital draw.; Operational resilience, commissioning flexibility, contingency adequacy and lifecycle certainty.

scenario_matrix.label: Lower Risk
scenario_matrix.why: You bought confidence by adding assurance, float and procurement evidence.; Reduced downside volatility and stronger approval defensibility, at the expense of market timing.; Market-entry acceleration, lean capex posture and near-term strategic timing.

scenario_matrix.label: Premium
scenario_matrix.why: You purchased operational separation, redundancy and downside protection capacity.; Higher mission resilience, commissioning separation and stronger downside containment.; Lean capital optics and minimum-approval positioning.

outputs_board_memo: Base is the reference case: no cost, schedule or confidence delta is applied. Use Faster, Cheaper, Lower Risk or Premium to expose the board trade-off.
Scenario trade: Balanced cost, time and evidence posture.
What you gained: Maintains a credible reference case for board challenge.
What you gave up: Does not buy extra time certainty or capital efficiency.
Board warning: Power availability and systems integration readiness are more likely to govern delivery than civil progress alone.
Decision rule: do not approve this scenario until the changed risk owner, reserve position and critical-path evidence are explicit.
top_decisions_required: Accept or reject the scenario trade-off explicitly at board level.
Confirm the changed critical path and near-critical path density.
Approve the scenario-specific reserve and contingency philosophy.
Assign named owners for the new top scenario risks.
Confirm whether XER, cost workbook and risk register should be issued as scenario-controlled outputs.

export_scenario_control.xer_logic: critical path, duration and basis changed by scenario
export_scenario_control.cost_logic: CBS basis, reserve and uncertainty spread changed by scenario
export_scenario_control.risk_logic: probability, mitigation and scenario-emergent risks changed by scenario

live_calibration_summary: Current sector conditions are being applied to confidence, contingency and delivery-tail exposure.

live_calibration_signals.basis: Utility interconnection and energisation remain the dominant delivery constraints.
Long-lead electrical packages shape procurement tail exposure.
Cooling readiness and heat-rejection capacity govern commissioning risk.
Integrated systems testing and phased data-hall readiness drive confidence.

live_calibration_strip: Grid connection congestion • Transformer / switchgear lead times • Liquid cooling readiness • IST and phased hall turnover

audit_spine.detail: Headline, executive narrative, QCRA/QSRA, risk register and exports use the same selected-scenario payload.
Cost P80 and QSRA P80 86 months are visible for board challenge.
Board posture remains tied to evidence closure, not dashboard colour.
